title: Image can't be pushed to an openshift docker registry
body:
## Issue

Image can't be pushed to an openshift docker registry

```
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:fb15b60ae93220334096891a970266fad8dbd924c154310edefdb87e20ccc272
Copying blob sha256:831a05810227c179b57ecfaaccefa54c547c437661948b072a78032f265ceed2
Copying blob sha256:34aeb4de17e9e89f8fb39707f12bf239c8315f1bea2d86e04071bfb8ccc8e306
Copying blob sha256:2411e39d1cd02a52d9ad8d262362e9dcfc73bcd5cc188c10970080f82e6f57aa
Copying blob sha256:050c734bd2868bcd3b69ab0ca033aa3bc95a00a4a1e5317e732394e1c36ef59e
Copying blob sha256:b0d0ebbfa0e36e604afe61ca8e2af9cadd6c27636a469462ae24e6ac649e739d
Copying blob sha256:e9300633a9ba3610939733a35a62e05a5276e21ad0d087ec9a536c3163b3761d
Copying blob sha256:00e2cec343cd44986119abe635de29f3a09aef58a640dae38c94f49dc2fd4a9d
error copying layers and metadata from "containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.imagestore=/var/lib/shared,overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs,overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on]docker-registry.default.svc:5000/test1/fruit-backend-sb:latest" to "docker://docker-registry.default.svc:5000/test1/fruit-backend-sb:latest": 
Error writing blob: Error initiating layer upload to /v2/test1/fruit-backend-sb/blobs/uploads/ in
docker-registry.default.svc:5000: unauthorized: authentication required
```

Command executed : https://github.com/snowdrop/component-operator/blob/569911f2ea615030e44553624348a03adc8f4f1f/pkg/controller/component/tasks.go#L128-L147
```
Name:  "push",
Image: "quay.io/openshift-pipeline/buildah",
Command: []string{
	"buildah",
},
Args: []string{
	"push",
	"--tls-verify=${inputs.params.verifyTLS}",
	"${outputs.resources.image.url}",
	"docker://${outputs.resources.image.url}",
},
VolumeMounts: []corev1.VolumeMount{
	{
		MountPath: "/var/lib/containers",
		Name:      "libcontainers"},
},
SecurityContext: &corev1.SecurityContext{
	Privileged: newTrue(),
},
```

The certificate of the openshift server is well registered as docker cert on the VM running the ocp cluster 3.11

```
s -la /etc/docker/certs.d/docker-registry.default.svc\:5000/
total 8
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 4096 Sep 18  2018 .
drwxr-xr-x. 7 root root 4096 Jun 20 10:37 ..
lrwxrwxrwx. 1 root root   30 Sep 18  2018 node-client-ca.crt -> /etc/origin/node/client-ca.crt
[root@CentOS-74-64-minimal ~]# cat /etc/docker/certs.d/docker-registry.default.svc\:5000/node-client-ca.crt
-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----
MIIC6jCCAdKgAwIBAgIBATANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQsFADAmMSQwIgYDVQQDDBtvcGVu
c2hpZnQtc2lnbmVyQDE1NDMyMTA3NjQwHhcNMTgxMTI2MDUzOTI0WhcNMjMxMTI1

gPO/qoc93gG1lQlTBhzkb6ERVcXemiohkqvxzO60wuW8pKh7EpvGKSNdumFjZKUB

K/rt2Q+00ZcCtEp6ZayF4YqqakZkzID+vGDjABbSPDHrt7HIra7PgoJ4jOz1SUBo

-----END CERTIFICATE-----
```

comments.body: Buildah inside of the container would not have access to the /etc/docker/certs.d on the host?  I would think you need to volume mount it into the container for this to work.

Problem resolved after changing the RBAC rule 

```
APIGroups:     []string{"image.openshift.io"},
Resources:     []string{"imagestreams","imagestreams/layers"},
Verbs:         []string{"*"},
```

to be used for the serviceaccount executing the command `push`
